content.full_text:
,
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 30, 1990

David Bauer
David Bauer Business Services
1121 L Street, Suite 1000
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-90-626

Dear Mr. Bauer:

You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act.¹ We do not have sufficient facts to
provide a definitive answer to your question; therefore, we
consider your letter to be a request for informal assistance
pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy enclosed).²

QUESTION

How would Team for the '90's, a slate mailer organization,
report receipt of payments from the telephone company for a "900"
phone line established to provide endorsements of ballot measures,
and how would it report such endorsements?

CONCLUSION

If Team for the '90's does not qualify as a major donor
committee or independent expenditure committee, it will have no
requirement to report expenditures or payments received from the telephone
company, nor expenditures made on behalf of ballot measures.
However, if Team for the '90's is making payments at the behest of
a recipient committee primarily formed to support or oppose a
ballot measure, the affected ballot measure committee must report
receiving non-monetary contributions from Team for the '90's.

In the case of a slate mailer organization which makes
$10,000 or more in contributions in a calendar year to candidates
or committees, including non-monetary contributions, the
organization must report as a major donor committee. If the slate
mailer organization does not qualify as a major donor but makes
$1,000 or more in independent expenditures, it must report as an
independent expenditure committee.

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

David Bauer
Page Two

FACTS

Team for the '90's, a slate mailer organization, will be
sending out a slate mailer for the November 1990 elections. On
the slate mailer will be printed a "900" telephone number which
members of the public may call to receive additional endorsements.
The additional endorsements will be for ballot measures. Team for
the '90's will not receive any payments for the purpose of setting
up the "900" number.

To establish the "900" telephone line, Team for the '90's
will make a deposit to the telephone company for costs associated
with setting up the line. When customers use the number, they
will be billed for the call on their regular telephone bills. The
telephone company, after receiving payment from the customers,
will deduct a fee for its services and then remit the balance to
Team for the '90's.

ANALYSIS

When a slate mailer organization (SMO) is required to file a
campaign statement under Section 84218, the organization must
report, in part, its receipts and payments made for the production
or distribution of slate mailers. (Section 84219.) Since the
payments Team for the '90's would make to establish a "900" line
and the payments it would receive from the public's use of the
line would not be "for the production or distribution of slate
mailers," none of these receipts or disbursements need to be
reported on the Form 401.

However, if Team for the '90's makes independent expenditures
of $1,000 or more in a calendar year to support or oppose
candidates or ballot measures, it will qualify as an independent
expenditure Committee. (Section 82013(b).) In addition, if it
makes contributions totaling $10,000 or more in a calendar year to
candidates or committees, it will qualify as a major donor
committee. (Section 82031(c).)

An independent expenditure is

....an expenditure made by any person in connection with a
communication which expressly advocates the election or
defeat of a clearly identified candidate or the
qualification, passage or defeat of clearly identified
measure, or taken as a whole and in context unambiguously
urges a particular result in an election but which is not
made to or at the behest of the affected candidate or
committee.

Section 82031.

David Bauer
Page Three

"At the behest" means:

...a payment made under the control or at the direction of,
in cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert with,
or at the request or suggestion of a candidate, controlled
committee, official committee of a political party, or
organization formed or existing primarily for political
purposes.

Regulation 18215(c).

"Contribution" means:

...a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a loan by
a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a payment
except to the extent that full and adequate consideration is
received unless it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not made for political purposes. An
expenditure made at the behest of a candidate, committee or
elected officer is a contribution to the candidate, committee
or elected officer unless full and adequate consideration is
received for making the expenditure.

Section 82015.

Team for the '90's costs associated with the establishment of
the "900" line and the on-going costs per call, including the
amount retained by the telephone company, must be calculated. To
calculate independent expenditures, the SMO should use a
proportional share of the costs associated with establishing the
"900" line and the on-going costs. To calculate contributions,
the total costs involved should be attributed to each ballot
measure committee at whose behest the SMO is making payments to
establish the "900" line.

To determine the amount of expenditures Team for the '90's
makes in connection with the "900" line, the costs for
establishment of the "900" line and the on-going costs per call,
including the amount retained by the telephone company, must be
calculated.

If the expenditures for the "900" line are "independent
expenditures" (i.e., they are not made at the behest of a
committee), the amount of the independent expenditure attributable
to each ballot measure is the proportional share of all costs in
connection with the "900" line.

If the expenditures are "contributions" (they are made at the
behest of a ballot measure committee), the total costs in
connection with establishing the line, as well as the proportion
of the cost per call (the amount retained by the telephone
company) attributable to each ballot measure will be considered a
contribution to each ballot measure committee at whose behest Team
for the '90's has made an endorsement.

David Bauer
Page Four

     If Team for the '90's qualifies as either a major donor
committee or an independent expenditure committee, all
contributions and independent expenditures must be disclosed using
Form 461 (Independent Expenditure and Major Donor Committee
Campaign Statement). In addition, because $500 or more has been
expended on each ballot measure supported or opposed by
independent expenditures, a Supplemental Independent Expenditure
Report (Form 465) must be filed for each such ballot measure.
Please refer to the enclosed Information Manual E for the
reporting obligations of both an independent expenditure committee
and a major donor committee.

     If you have additional questions, please call me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
                                        Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

If Team for the '90's does not qualify as a major donor committee or independent expenditure committee, it will have no requirement to report payments received from the telephone company, nor expenditures made on behalf of ballot measures. However, if Team for the '90's is making payments at the behest of a recipient committee primarily formed to support or oppose a ballot measure, the affected ballot measure committee must report receiving non-monetary contributions from Team for the '90's.

In the case of a slate mailer organization which makes $10,000 or more in contributions in a calendar year to candidates or committees, including non-monetary contributions, the organization must report as a major donor committee. If the slate mailer organization does not qualify as a major donor but makes $1,000 or more in independent expenditures, it must report as an independent expenditure committee.

¹, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title

However, if Team for the '90's makes independent expenditures of $1,000 or more in a calendar year to support or oppose candidates or ballot measures, it will qualify as an independent expenditure committee. Regulation 18215(c).

"Contribution" means:

...a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a loan by a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a payment except to the extent that full and adequate consideration is received unless it is clear from the surrounding circumstances that it is not made for political purposes.
